block.text: Quality standard: Untitled — Every article must meet this standard before publish.
Add these internal links across your articles to boost technical authority and guide readers through key concepts. Use them in intros, "See also" boxes, and at section ends.
Applying Pump Affinity Laws: From Theory to Real‑World Applications
block.link: Applying Pump Affinity Laws: From Theory to Real‑World Applications

block.text: The Essential Guide to Pressure Drop Calculations in Piping Systems
block.link: The Essential Guide to Pressure Drop Calculations in Piping Systems

block.text: Pipe Sizing in FluidFlow: Methods, Principles, and Best Practices
block.link: Pipe Sizing in FluidFlow: Methods, Principles, and Best Practices

block.text: What is Cavitation — and How Do You Avoid It in Fluid Systems
block.link: What is Cavitation — and How Do You Avoid It in Fluid Systems

block.text: Net Positive Suction Head (NPSH) in Pump Systems
block.link: Net Positive Suction Head (NPSH) in Pump Systems

block.text: Calculation Methods and Standards Used in FluidFlow
block.link: Calculation Methods and Standards Used in FluidFlow

block.text: Fluid Databases and Mixing Capabilities in FluidFlow
block.link: Fluid Databases and Mixing Capabilities in FluidFlow

block.text: Engineering Slurry Flow Systems: Principles, Challenges, and Solutions
block.link: Engineering Slurry Flow Systems: Principles, Challenges, and Solutions

block.text: Glossary pages to link for better user journeys
Getting Started articles: add "Next steps" linking to System Curve, Pump Affinity Laws, and Pipe Sizing.
Installation/Licensing: add CTA to FAQs and Glossary — Valves, Pipes.
Technical Library topics: add "See also" to Pressure Drop, Cavitation, NPSH as applicable.
Industry pages: cross‑link to Pipe Sizing, Pressure Drop, and relevant component glossary.
Troubleshooting: link to Cavitation, NPSH, Pressure Drop.
Clusters are a gateway to the content, and reinforce the blogs for search, also, more of a journey if they are interlinked together. The KB will act as conversion content, blog awareness
New docs in that page are blogs we have not covered but should have done, we can figure out where to put them in.
Page is a bit of a mess, we can delete some stuff from it to make it easier to manage.
I like the structure you have suggested but suggest including the following:

I like the idea of blog in Knowledgebase, however we have the blog.fluidflowinfo.com for awareness pieces, but, if we make FF heavy blog pieces and how-to guides in the KB they become more conversion content
We should extend some topics from the help file.
Each article is numbered P01–P144 by priority. Work through batches sequentially. Every piece must pass the Untitled before publish.
Numbering convention: P{nn} = global priority · (§n) = section on this page where the draft lives.
P02 · Build Your First Network in FluidFlow (§1)

Getting Started section required for both FF and FD but with different content/structure
Duplicate the FF KB Structure Notion Page for FD - DONE
Installation and Licensing section required for both FF and FD but with different content/structure
Low Priority for FF, High Priority for FD
Apps Team to conduct a separate brainstorming activity to finalize the content of this section in FD
FF and FD can be done simultaneously
We can create this post-release, for AI, for Chatbot, etc.)
FD: To be included in FD Glossary
FD (TBD) - will be finalized post-release once the Pre-Calculation Checklist is available
Glossary required for both FF and FD
